name: Summer Vegetable Soup
recipeIngredient: 4 medium carrots (1/4 pound), peeled and sliced
1 pound green or other snap beans
2 cups torn beet greens or spinach, grated (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. In an 8-quart saucepot, heat olive oil and butter over medium heat. Add carrots, celery, and onion; sauté for 3 minutes. Stir in garlic; sauté for 1 minute. Add water, Worcestershire sauce, salt, pepper, and bay leaf; cover and bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to low and simmer vegetable mixture for 15 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, snap the stem end off each bean and discard. Cut beans into 1-inch lengths. Peel and cut potatoes into 1/2-inch cubes. Cut squash lengthwise into quarters, then crosswise into 1/4-inch-thick slices.
3. Remove and discard bay leaf from vegetable mixture. Add beans, potatoes, and squash to the vegetable mixture; bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to low and cook until potatoes are tender—about 10 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, peel and chop tomatoes. Stir tomatoes and beet greens into vegetable mixture; cook until greens are wilted—about 1 minute. Ladle soup into serving bowls; top with Parmesan cheese if desired. Serve immediately.
